prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

BTC being above 70,000 on the April 20 Polymarket contract remains more likely than not, but the best post-synthesis read is slightly below the market’s 0.86 implied probability because the contract resolves on one exact Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close and the independent truth-finding pass mostly confirmed current cushion and catalyst absence rather than proving that a sub-70k print is remote.

## Why this may matter now

Market implies 0.86. My final syndicated range is 0.80-0.85. That makes the edge versus market marginal-to-modest and probably not robust enough to call strong alpha. The likely mispricing, if any, is that the market may still be a bit too comfortable treating a 6%+ cushion as near-lock protection despite the contract resolving on one exact Binance minute close.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

This is only slightly above the swarm-implied center, not a major break from it. I moved a bit upward from the provisional median because the synthesis-stage verification confirmed the specific catalyst-hunter point that no FOMC decision or CPI print sits inside the remaining window, and a fresh Binance check still showed BTC around 74.5k. I di …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market's 86% Yes price is broadly justified by BTC trading well above 70k, but I discount slightly for narrow timestamp risk and normal five-day crypto volatility.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's 86% prior and ask whether the current price cushion makes that level reasonable.
Verify contract mechanics and confirm the governing source is Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close, strictly above 70k.
Check direct Binance spot data and recent daily closes; BTC is comfortably above 70k now and has recently held above it.
Discount slightly because the contract is a narrow timestamp event rather than a broad end-of-day condition.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot during research was around 74.5k, materially above the 70k threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes in the sampled week all remained above 70k.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact one-minute close, so a >6% downside move before noon ET April 20 is enough to flip the result.
Crypto can move that magnitude within five days, especially on fresh macro or crypto-specific stress.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether realized volatility between now and April 20 is low enough to fully justify an 86% price rather than a slightly lower one.
How much latent event risk the market may already be incorporating beyond visible public spot context.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC loses most of its cushion toward 70k, if downside volatility spikes, or if Binance-specific issues emerge; I would move closer to market if BTC stays comfortably above the mid-74k area into the weekend.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: This is a date-sensitive, timezone-specific market resolved at noon ET on April 20 using a single Binance one-minute close.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Source quality is medium-high: contract mechanics are clear, Binance is the governing venue for direct price context, and CoinGecko offered a useful but limited independent cross-check.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes remains more likely than No, but the market likely overprices confidence because this resolves on one exact Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at noon ET and current cushion above 70k is meaningful but not huge for BTC over five days.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves on a very specific Binance BTC/USDT minute-close condition rather than on a broad market narrative.
Current Binance pricing sits comfortably above 70k, so Yes is still the base case.
But the remaining five-day horizon plus BTC volatility make the market's 86% confidence look somewhat rich.
That supports a modestly below-market Yes estimate rather than a full bearish call.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance checks during the run showed BTC/USDT around 74.6k, above 70k.
Polymarket rules explicitly settle on Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close, making timing/path risk materially relevant.
strongest_disconfirmers: Even the recent 24-hour Binance low remained above 70k, which supports the market's high Yes probability.
No strong imminent bearish catalyst was identified in this run.
unresolved_ambiguities: Operational details of the exact Binance display surface at settlement are narrow but not materially ambiguous after the rule/API check.
No independent volatility model was built in this run.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move toward or above market if BTC retains a large cushion above 70k with declining volatility into April 20, and more bearish if BTC starts revisiting low-72k/71k levels or a downside catalyst appears.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Resolution depends on the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle close at exactly 12:00 ET on April 20; timezone mapping was explicitly checked against Binance server time.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-value direct evidence from the named resolution source plus clear contract wording; evidence independence is only moderate because both rule interpretation and verification center on Binance.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is already materially above 70,000 on the named Binance venue, and with no obvious top-tier scheduled macro catalyst left before Apr 20 noon ET, the most likely path is simply staying above the threshold unless an unscheduled downside shock hits.
main_logical_chain: Check the contract mechanics and confirm the governing source is Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close above 70,000.
Verify that current Binance spot is materially above 70,000 and recent closes have mostly held above that threshold.
Check whether any top-tier scheduled macro catalyst remains before resolution; official calendars suggest not.
Conclude that the dominant risk is an unscheduled downside shock rather than a scheduled repricing event, leaving Yes slightly more likely than the market implies.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT was around 74.5k during the run, roughly 6% above the 70k threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes in the sampled output were above 70k for Apr 10-14.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact Binance 12:00 ET 1-minute close, so a localized dip below 70k is enough for No.
A 6% BTC move over five days is plausible in crypto.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether noon ET specifically could coincide with localized liquidity weakness on Binance.
Whether any missed or unscheduled catalyst emerges before Apr 20.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A decisive break below 72k on Binance, a new risk-off macro/geopolitical shock, or evidence of Binance-specific operational/pricing instability would lower the estimate.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Timing is central: the market is already in-the-money, so the key question is whether any catalyst pushes Binance BTC/USDT below 70,000 at the specific Apr 20 noon ET settlement minute.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Source quality is medium-high overall: Binance is the named direct source family, Polymarket provides explicit contract rules, and Fed/BLS calendars add independent official context on scheduled catalysts.
